text: Big Bend National Park
zh: 大本德国家公园

text: Chihuahuan Desert,
zh: 奇瓦瓦沙漠,

text: largest desert in North America, and the highest in elevation, reaches a thousand miles from the heart of Old Mexico into the southwestern United States.
zh: 是北美洲最大、海拔最高的沙漠,从古老墨西哥的心脏地带一直延伸近千英里,直至美国西南部。

text: Here in far west Texas, on the Mexican border, there is a land mass larger than the state of Rhode Island, isolated from civilization.
zh: 在得克萨斯州最西端、与墨西哥接壤的地方,有一片比罗德岛州还大的土地,与文明世界隔绝。

text: Big Bend National Park protects more than 5 ,000 species of living things.
zh: 大本德国家公园庇护着超过5000种生物。

text: A river, the Rio Grande.
zh: 一条河,格兰德河。

text: Loops from the Rockies in Colorado to the Gulf of Mexico.
zh: 它从科罗拉多州的落基山脉蜿蜒流向墨西哥湾。

text: Its flow bending through the desert tracts of Texas.
zh: 河水穿行在得克萨斯州的沙漠地带,曲折蜿蜒。

text: Texas.
zh: 得克萨斯。

text: Texans call it Big Bend country.
zh: 得克萨斯人称这里为大本德地区。

text: When Spanish explorers first saw it some centuries ago, they called it despoblado, the uninhabited land.
zh: 几个世纪前,西班牙探险家初次见到这里时,称其为despoblado,即“无人之地”。

text: They were wrong.
zh: 他们错了。

text: Today, it is the home of Big Bend National Park.
zh: 如今,这里是大本德国家公园的所在地。

text: Here in far west Texas, near the Mexican border, this remote The remote park is larger than the state of Rhode Island.
zh: 在得克萨斯州最西端、靠近墨西哥边境的地方,这座偏远的公园比罗德岛州还要大。

text: And it is inhabited by more than 5 ,000 species of living things.
zh: 这里栖息着超过5000种生物。

text: Not counting visitors who find the park very much alive with things to see and do.
zh: 这还不算那些发现公园里有无数景观和活动、生机勃勃的游客。

text: The river bends around the Chisos Mountains.
zh: 河流绕过奇索斯山脉蜿蜒而行。

text: Atop these peaks grow forests.
zh: 山顶之上生长着森林。

text: Trees which hold moisture and give shade from the punishing sun.
zh: 这些树木涵养水分,也为烈日提供庇荫。

text: Mountain slopes made white with winter snows.
zh: 冬雪覆盖下的山坡一片洁白。

text: Desert, yes.
zh: 没错,这里是沙漠。

text: But there are also desert grasslands and desert flowers.
zh: 但也有沙漠草原和沙漠野花。

text: And there's water.
zh: 而且这里有水。

text: in the flow of the Rio Grande.
zh: 就在格兰德河的水流之中。

text: For more than a thousand miles, this meandering river is the international boundary between two sovereign nations.
zh: 这条蜿蜒千余英里的河流,是两个主权国家之间的国际边界。

text: the USA and the Republic of Mexico.
zh: 美国与墨西哥合众国。

text: Big Bend National Park administers 244 miles of that boundary.
zh: 大本德国家公园管辖着这条边界中244英里的路段。

text: This border has not always been inviolate.
zh: 这条边界并非始终不可侵犯。

text: rustlers have raided here and bandits looted when pursued they
zh: 盗贼曾在此劫掠,土匪作案后一旦被追捕,

text: simply fled to Mexico across the river one notable outlaw was dorteo Arango a volatile mixture of Robin Hood and bandito
zh: 便径直渡河逃往墨西哥。其中一位著名的亡命之徒名叫多罗特奥·阿朗戈,是侠盗与土匪的混合体,

text: who was also known on both sides of the US Mexican border as Pancho Villa he was a brilliant horseman marksman and general.
zh: 在美墨边境两侧,他更广为人知的名字是潘乔·维拉。他骑术精湛,枪法出众,也是一位杰出的将领。

text: Because of changes in elevation, from cool mountainsides to dry, hot desert, the park abounds in different kinds of wildlife.
zh: 由于海拔变化——从凉爽的山坡到干燥炎热的沙漠——公园内野生动物种类繁多。

text: But most creatures withdraw into the shadows during the day, particularly in the desert.
zh: 但大多数动物白天都躲在阴影中,尤其是在沙漠里。

text: At night, they become wildly alive live and forage for food.
zh: 到了夜晚,它们才活跃起来,四处觅食。

text: Bobcats, coyotes,
zh: 短尾猫、丛林狼、

text: lizards, snakes, tortoises and turtles, and peccaries.
zh: 蜥蜴、蛇、陆龟与水龟,以及野猪。

text: With over 450 species in the park, birdwatching is a popular pursuit.
zh: 公园里鸟类超过450种,观鸟因此成为一项热门活动。

text: Big Bend is one of the largest, most remote national parks in the lower 48 states, which makes hiking there a real adventure.
zh: 大本德是美国本土48州中最大、最偏远的国家公园之一,这让徒步旅行在这里成为一场真正的冒险。

text: On trails blazed by landmarks, such as these two stone towers, nicknamed Mule Ears.
zh: 沿着地标指引的小径前行,比如这两座被昵称为“骡耳”的石塔。

text: Grapevine Hills Trail, an easy walk, leads to a group of balanced rocks.
zh: 葡萄藤山径是一条轻松易走的步道,通向一组平衡岩。

text: After a hike, tired adventurers can soak off fatigue in the naturally therapeutic waters of Big Bend's own hot springs.
zh: 徒步之后,疲惫的探险者可以在大本德自有的天然温泉中泡去疲劳,享受天然疗愈之水。

text: 118 miles of river ribbons the southern boundary of the park.
zh: 118英里长的河流如缎带般环绕着公园的南部边界。

text: A joy to visitors who want their canoeing skills challenged by paddling through spectacular Santa Elena Canyon.
zh: 对于希望挑战独木舟技巧的游客来说,穿越壮观的圣埃琳娜峡谷是一大乐事。

text: Sheer rock cliffs rise 1400 feet above the water.
zh: 陡峭的岩壁高耸于水面之上1400英尺。

text: one of the sights that places Big Bend among the most spectacular outdoor areas in North America.
zh: 这正是让大本德跻身北美最壮观户外胜地之列的景观之一。
